speaker: MATT
raw_text: I have a feeling Kern and Grog are going to have a round two in the near future, depending on how Grog's feeling about that. Then the party saw that there was some sort of hydra-like being that had been harrying the walls of the outskirts of the city of Vasselheim. Being the adventurers they are, they took it upon themselves to leap out into the jungles and the forests of the Vesper Timberland. Found and hunted down this hydra, fought it, and upon slaying it, discovered there was another group also hunting this hydra. An actually sanctioned group. And that hunting this hydra is considered illegal in Vasselheim unless you had actually been contracted to do so. So in breaking the laws of Vasselheim, they could have been tried for it, but instead chose to return to the city with this group of hunters and be brought before their guildmaster, the Huntmaster known as Huntmaster Vanessa Cyndrial. Basically, they were given the choice: they could go to trial or they could undergo their own personal trial to join the guild, the Slayer's Take, which is essentially a guild that takes on contracts to hunt various requested creatures down, harvest elements of their physical bodies to then be sold or returned to these individuals and be paid for it. If they manage to complete these trials, they will be absolved of breaking the law because they will now be within the confines of it and will be members of the guild. So choosing that, they were then also notified that they would be splitting up into two different groups, much to the chagrin of Vex and Vax, our twins.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Anyone else need a pencil? Everyone good?

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: That was a good throw and catch.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: I have a mechanical pencil that looks like a pencil pencil.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I think I have the same kind of pencil.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We do the hand-eye coordination thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty, guys. As it currently stands, Vox Machina throughout its ventures found itself in the city of Vasselheim, as of late. They came to seal away the Horn of Orcus they had tore from the head of the beholder K'Varn deep in the Underdark. Once they had completed this journey, they spent some time in the city trying to figure out what can be done there, preparation for possibly Keyleth making a venture out to one of her Ashari tribes. During which, Grog entered a fight ring and lost. And the party decided to go ahead and--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Never forget.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So this group, the second adventuring group, has gathered itself back to the Bellows' Respite inn. Vax, you were asked to go the bar, to join your sister, Vex, for a shot of the hardest liquor they had available to deal with the coming separation. You shared a brief breakfast, shared your bitter, spiteful, alcoholic imbibement, and then went your separate ways. The first group went initially, and it's been about an hour or so of you guys waiting in the tavern for them to, as far as you know, take care of their business before you were to go to the guild yourselves. So, we have Tiberius, Keyleth, and Vax all sitting around the Bellows' Respite tavern lamenting what's going to come next.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Split apart.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Separation anxiety is a real thing.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Right now, I'd say it's roughly 9 00 to 10:00 in the morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's only a few days. It'll be good. Separation makes the heart grow fonder and all that stuff.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's fine. I just have a low tolerance for bullshit, and I'd like to get past this little piece of shit that we're being made to swallow.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I agree. Stormwinds should not be meddling around with these peasants. Are we in a tavern?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You are currently in the Bellows' Respite Tavern.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Barkeep, bring me your finest ale for my friend here and for the lady. I'll take a water.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The relatively jovial, ginger-bearded, rotund human gentleman who runs the establishment comes over to you. Looks like himself is a little bleary-eyed and hasn't quite adjusted to the morning light itself. "Oh okay. Certainly, I'll go ahead and retrieve that for you. It's three? Three is what I'm looking at?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What time is it?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Five minutes go by. Your food and drink are brought to your table, and you continue to lament about the coming time. Is there anything else you guys wish to take care of before you make your way to the Slayer's Take Guildhall?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I agree. Stormwinds should not be meddling around with these peasants. Are we in a tavern?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You are currently in the Bellows' Respite Tavern.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Barkeep, bring me your finest ale for my friend here and for the lady. I'll take a water.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The relatively jovial, ginger-bearded, rotund human gentleman who runs the establishment comes over to you. Looks like himself is a little bleary-eyed and hasn't quite adjusted to the morning light itself. "Oh okay. Certainly, I'll go ahead and retrieve that for you. It's three? Three is what I'm looking at?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What time is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Right now, I'd say it's roughly 9 00 to 10:00 in the morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh sure. Let's start early. Why not?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: And I flip him a couple of gold coins.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He tries to catch it, fumbles. Goes and pick it up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Don't throw. That's rude.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 16? Okay. As you guys take the next 20 minutes wandering the Quad Roads district of Vasselheim, asking around. A lot of the shops here offer general supplies. There are a few that are focused on the mining supplies as there is a large market mining cobalt out of the center of the mountain the city is built around. You find places that provide clockwork for large grandfather clocks that are starting to be distributed in this part of the city. You also find that there is a large bazaar-type market in the center of the Quad Roads that you passed by when you first came to town. Amongst many of these barkers, you do catch one small tent, Tiberius, that catches your eye because it resembles the same purple color as one tent you guys entered in Emon about a year or so ago, in which you encountered a fortune teller.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He said, remember, you took the flying carpet. To break RP for a second.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (imitating Grog) Am I texting you that right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. I can't. You're cutting out. You're cutting out, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I held it for a time, but I only have my Wheel of Mending in mine, as far as I know. Hold on, let me check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you reach into your Bag of Holding--

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I'm thinking really hard.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Through the luck of the draw, it seems to be that the last resting place of the flying carpet was within your Bag of Holding.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Hold on. Oh here it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, look at that. We have it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Shut my mouth. I guess I was wrong.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Found it. We're good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, let's go to the store. I don't really want to drag this out any longer than we have to. And I walk out the door and start going.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. What did you roll for your investigation check, Tiberius?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 16? Okay. As you guys take the next 20 minutes wandering the Quad Roads district of Vasselheim, asking around. A lot of the shops here offer general supplies. There are a few that are focused on the mining supplies as there is a large market mining cobalt out of the center of the mountain the city is built around. You find places that provide clockwork for large grandfather clocks that are starting to be distributed in this part of the city. You also find that there is a large bazaar-type market in the center of the Quad Roads that you passed by when you first came to town. Amongst many of these barkers, you do catch one small tent, Tiberius, that catches your eye because it resembles the same purple color as one tent you guys entered in Emon about a year or so ago, in which you encountered a fortune teller.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He said, remember, you took the flying carpet. To break RP for a second.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (imitating Grog) Am I texting you that right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. I can't. You're cutting out. You're cutting out, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I held it for a time, but I only have my Wheel of Mending in mine, as far as I know. Hold on, let me check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you reach into your Bag of Holding--

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I'm thinking really hard.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Through the luck of the draw, it seems to be that the last resting place of the flying carpet was within your Bag of Holding.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Hold on. Oh here it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, look at that. We have it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Shut my mouth. I guess I was wrong.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Found it. We're good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, let's go to the store. I don't really want to drag this out any longer than we have to. And I walk out the door and start going.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. What did you roll for your investigation check, Tiberius?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "It's good to see you again, young half-elf." You can see now with the current atmosphere, the light seems to dim the interior of the tent. And the elven woman's visage shimmers and then fades and there sitting behind the table you see a large, hunched forward, gnarled humanoid. This woman, who is definitively referred to as a hag, is currently lording over the table, long, twisted fingers holding that same deck of cards. Looking up at you with a crooked nose and pointed chin. There's a grin on her face. And looks to you and, "It has been some time, dragonborn. I can still see there is a fraction of you still missing."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Am I with him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're behind him and outside of the tent. He entered. Did you guys want to go in with him?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, you enter and are greeted by this same atmosphere.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (gasps) Trista!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She gives you a cross look.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's been so long! Fancy meeting you here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She puts her finger up for a second, turns over to the noblewoman who is currently perusing this small shelf of books and tomes. "I'm terribly sorry. Would you mind just giving us a moment? I've some personal business to attend to." The noble woman is like, "Oh, fine. I didn't want to purchase anything anyway." And stomps out of the tent. The door closes behind her. At which point the elven woman immediately turns. "Trista's my sister."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You're Forsythia?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "As you know, my dealings with fate take a lot out of me. I'm not quite ready for another round of tellings. You see, this city, as most bits of civilization, are very eager to hear what the future has in store for them. So I'm a bit tapped out at the moment." And you can see a bit of dried blood around one of her nostrils. And if you recall last time, the process was very physically taxing on her. "Perhaps return in a week or so. And if I'm not here, I'm around."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I know something bad is going to happen. I show them the box. I take it and pick it up. This is all she had to offer. She's not taking questions.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You're not taking any questions right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Not today."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Not today?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: She stands up and reaches over, and there's a small alchemical glassware set up on the side. And you see it's cracked and old and it's been brutalized. And she pulls a very small vial of some dark, black, almost umbra-type liquid where you can't even see shine in the light. It absorbs all the light. She puts it forward. "This would be very unfortunate to find its way into the bloodstream of an enemy, yes?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You're not taking any questions right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Not today."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Not today?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "As you know, my dealings with fate take a lot out of me. I'm not quite ready for another round of tellings. You see, this city, as most bits of civilization, are very eager to hear what the future has in store for them. So I'm a bit tapped out at the moment." And you can see a bit of dried blood around one of her nostrils. And if you recall last time, the process was very physically taxing on her. "Perhaps return in a week or so. And if I'm not here, I'm around."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Old one, are you still willing to trade today?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Trade?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm wondering if you have any poisons beyond the run-of-the-mill nightshades this afternoon or morning?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Yes, they left not more than an hour ago, I imagine."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: They already headed out?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: She looks around, and points off. And you can see, there isn't a whole lot of Bastion presence in this portion of the city, especially since the Bastions are more of a voluntary guard. People from the different clergies, the different temples, train to become Bastions, and fit themselves with whatever their deity's armor is. You do see there are two Bastions armored in a very dark, dark brown leather that carry a symbol of what looks like a silver raven that is embroidered on one shoulder of a cloak that is tossed to one side.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I would agree.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Have you had invaders like this before?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I mean, death is not an uncommon occurrence for certain areas of the city." And she gestures off to the distant temple. "Some people invite it, even. But such violent death like this is usually kept to the outside of the city, at least with some sort of cause. This seems to be a murderous rampage that, me personally, I don't want to go out at night without protection."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is there is any indication of the cause of death on these corpses? How do they find them?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't know. I've not investigated. Perhaps you have to ask the Bastions yourself that. Or anyone who was in the vicinity."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And can you point us in the right direction?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. A few moments pass as you wonder where your dwarven leader has vanished to, he steps out through the front of the establishment.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes, of course, friend dwarf." And she goes and rummages through a small bag and pulls out a mask. This little purple mask that seems to have small fake gems forming the outside, with a couple of ribbons that dangle where it would tie around the head. It's simple, but it's pretty. "Unfortunately, not many people bring children to this side of the district. They don't sell very well. But if you would like one, I would be happy to part with for two gold?"

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: One gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a persuasion roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "One gold. Guess it is better to sell it than not at all. Here you are. I must say, I won't tell anyone, but purple is a pretty color on you."

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Yes, it is. He walks out.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Now, we have found a series of bodies over the past few years, scattered about. A few months at a time between. All with a similar pattern of death to them. We found large portions, the meaty portions of the body had been torn away, some of them apparently eaten. There are marks that are indicative of some sort of beast ravaging the corpses, but no signs of a beast coming or going. And we've had many of our finest trackers looking for any sort of sign of a creature that may have found its way into the city. They've been in the rooftops, the gutters, perhaps even the sewers. No sign of any such creature traveling amongst any of these scenes. Now, all of the bodies have been found wrapped up and discarded in the canals. A lot of them surrounding the Abundant Terrace." Looks at the rest of you. "That's where Melora's eye is watchful. What's really got us confused of late is, the most recent murder that we found was a very powerful merchant lord from Ank'harel, one that's been in this city for no more than a month. He's well-known in these parts as bringing in a lot of business. His caravans come through via various skyships. And he brings with himself an influx of much-needed mercantile in the city."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Fair enough.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: All right.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Now, we have found a series of bodies over the past few years, scattered about. A few months at a time between. All with a similar pattern of death to them. We found large portions, the meaty portions of the body had been torn away, some of them apparently eaten. There are marks that are indicative of some sort of beast ravaging the corpses, but no signs of a beast coming or going. And we've had many of our finest trackers looking for any sort of sign of a creature that may have found its way into the city. They've been in the rooftops, the gutters, perhaps even the sewers. No sign of any such creature traveling amongst any of these scenes. Now, all of the bodies have been found wrapped up and discarded in the canals. A lot of them surrounding the Abundant Terrace." Looks at the rest of you. "That's where Melora's eye is watchful. What's really got us confused of late is, the most recent murder that we found was a very powerful merchant lord from Ank'harel, one that's been in this city for no more than a month. He's well-known in these parts as bringing in a lot of business. His caravans come through via various skyships. And he brings with himself an influx of much-needed mercantile in the city."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Fair enough.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: All right.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Tyrell. Lord Tyrell. If you say any of this to any of our people, I swear I'll make your life the hardest it's been in months. And from the looks of you, you've had a very hard few months. Lord Tyrell's body was found, but apparently during the murder. We have one beggar that apparently caught sight of this the murderer. As best as we can tell, it was some sort of lycanthrope. The beggar was babbling, but it seemed there was some sort of wolf-like creature. It was seen bipedal walking a man, but tore this man right through the throat, most of his chest. But when spotted, didn't run, just vanished. Disappeared. No longer in his sight. No tracks left behind. Just gone. Didn't know if it was angry poltergeist, ghost. But the last place he was staying is at a recent establishment, only been here a couple of years. Referred to as the Velvet Cabaret. The Velvet Cabaret is a very high-end place."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What's his name?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm not allowed to say."

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: You're not allowed to say?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The merchant's name is Tyrell."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You have three days to complete the contract, meaning slaying the entity you're contracted to.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: This killer could be hiding in plain sight at the Cabaret. Could be an employee there.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Or the owner himself.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How long ago did the bastions say that they raided the Velvet Cabaret?

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: It was a month ago, wasn't it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It was a few weeks back when the murder occurred, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So here's my question  do you think he would stick around after it was raided? Or do you think he'd move on?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The thing is vanishing upon sight.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: And gotten away with it for the last two or three years. I don't think he cares much anymore.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: My question is  we have three days, Cabaret's the only lead we have. How do we draw this person, he or she, out?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I believe it is three days.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We have three days to collar it.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Wrath of the gods comes down upon you. All right, so you guys taken a few minutes to acquire outfits that seem a little more fitting as to the establishment you're planning to go and enter.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: I was talking Liam's ear off during the break about how much I love the dynamic among the three of you, and commiserating about how you feel really naked and exposed when your whole party isn't together. How weird that feels to not have everybody together at the same time. I think I kept him from the bathroom.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, gotcha. That's okay. He'll arrive. When he comes, we'll all awkwardly stare. Yeah. When he does. When he arrives we'll all just-- But in the meantime, you managed to go back and--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Stealth. Disadvantage on my next five attacks. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I love the awkward stare. It was great.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: He's actually a very generous DM if he does that. I actually make my players roll damage when they upset me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's good to know.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, dude, d6 damage if you annoy the GM.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. You guys make your way to a little bit further north this of this route, five or six blocks until you get the outskirts of the Velvet Cabaret, which based on the description you've gotten from a few individuals you asked around, very easy to find. The fancy house of entertainment. It is a two-story building of dark cherry wood with strips of what looks to be black suede bolted in a pattern along the outside of it. It stands out like a sore thumb against the rest of the architecture, but it looks a night club. It looks very, very gaudy, but in a way that the garishness looks expensive, and so you can't really fault it for looking the way it does.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We cater mostly to halflings and gnomes. Everyone, but specifically because they're so underserved.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: You have a gnome shoe empire?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Rolling in it, baby.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Where's your empire, human?

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I have no empire. I'm his valet.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're wonderful partners, dear.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Am I your wife?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to be? Is that weird?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I say it's equally-- no, it's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, it's fine. Yeah, I can be your wife. I can RP this. I mean I can pretend to be. Yeah. So Coralin Shorthalt.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Thorbir is pulling on his beard and breathing deeply.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Now I cast Alter Self.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Cast and a flash of light. You guys all pick up the very, very heavy smell of breakfast ale emanating from the deeply breathing, angry dwarf.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Wait, Vax. What was my role in this?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You're an advisor. A financial advisor.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Financial advisor. Excellent.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: He claps his hands, and you can see one of the servants that you saw walking with a small tray enters from the side of the hallway. "Yes?" Hosin turns. "The finest wine we have available for the elven lass right here." The servant immediately darts off, averting the eyes. A moment passes. Returns with three glasses of wine, looking over you two. Hands one to each of you. And the servant, still averting his gaze from the guests, smiles, gives a little curtsy, and then darts back into the main room.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Question, Hosin. While we wait, do you happen to have any wine or ale? Just while we wait. It's been such a long travel.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "But of course."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you. You're so kind.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I will tell you one thing that I am interested in buying. I'm interested in buying information. I'm interested in buying loyalty. I'm interested in buying trust. Since you've walked into this room, there has been an air of something not right about you. And through this conversation, I have come to believe that perhaps you're not telling me the truth. Now, I will ask of you this moment, whoever you may be, Shorthalt or not, but this entire facility is very well paid to make sure that should you try anything funny, you won't get but two steps before the heads leave your shoulders. So let's be frank here. Who are you? Why are you here?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The shoes are the public face, but the well runs deep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We get to the meat of the matter, eh? So, shoes is but an appearance. What is your business aside from cobbling, Mr. Shorthalt?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I know my way around poison, and I hear you have the rarest of breeds here. I have come to spend some money to satisfy my clients.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a deception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 15 again.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What was it?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 15 again.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Poisons? So you supply to what? King-killers? Supply to sell-swords? You apply them to your own weapons? You realize this city is very, very tight-knit when it comes to these sorts of industries. Although I know you would think possibly to find it here, I guarantee you what we deal with here has nothing to do with slitting of throats."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm not looking to sell. I'm looking to buy.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: We're currently hitting the one minute 15 second mark. Haven't quite pulled it out of the wood yet.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I turn my head to everyone and just mouth the words-- and perception check the room.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can I perception check, as well?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can I just look to see, I want to just meditate and-- what did you just roll? 24?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I want to see if I see any movements or brushes of cloth or anything like that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: 19. Not as good as Vax.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: How long does it take me to pull the axe out of the wall, and how far into Yakety Sax does the soundtrack get while I'm up there and with the foot and then I'm jumping on it and trying to pull it down.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. As you do, even with the encumbrance of the armor you're wearing, you feel like your form is a little more agile and you're ready for anything that might try and stop your passage. You guys begin making your way down, with Thorbir leading the way. You descend for a good three to four minutes, you gather probably a hundred feet or more down, rung by rung, and there are a few that you reach that are a little rickety. About a hundred feet down, one hits your foot and snaps, but you manage to catch yourself on it, but you get the feeling this ladder is probably not meant to hold someone of your heavy physical form.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: I'll go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. So we'll put in order here, who's going down next? All right. And after Vax?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll follow my husband.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth. That's weird. Tiberius?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Before we go down, I'm going to take off my outer-- no, I can't take off anything because that's all my gear. Never mind. I don't do that, but before we go down I cast Stoneskin on myself. I cast that right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. As your form shimmers for a second and you see-- you notice this too, Kashaw-- his scales almost seem to protrude into earthy spikes and then form and solidify, almost like his body becomes its own suit of armor.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Okay, that's cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Even though I'm still a dwarf, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to go, but I'm going to cast Freedom of Movement on myself before I go down there.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Well, Alter Self is gone if you're casting Stoneskin, because that's a concentration spell. Oh, that. But that only carries it for a few rounds. It's been a longer time than that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Run!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys glance over your shoulder and you see what looks to be a fairly rounded boulder--

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I dispel Alter Self immediately so I have longer-ass legs.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: And it's a terrible smell. This room is quite honestly a mixture of long-rotting food, of dried vegetation that has been discarded here for whatever reason, a lot of it's hay and thatch that has been just rotting and molding for god knows how many years, and quite honestly, feces from some indeterminate entity.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I'm laughing, by the way.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Can I try to move, since I hear him laughing, can I try to move out of the way?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You roll out of the way as the two of you scream down. With the light that now shines you can see this is a cylindrical cistern of some kind. It is a tunnel that is very tall, and you both impact into the same heap.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Shitter's full!

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: As we land, I look around. Oh, that was a lot of fun. I cast Prestidigitation three times in a row and clean us all up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You try and clean what you can, but you guys are like knee to nearly waist deep as you get up and move around--

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Or at least our faces.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Your faces are clean.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: What a wonderful smell you've discovered.
